recipes.name: Chicken Tacos
questions: Do you have corn tortillas, flour tortillas, or lettuce for wraps?
Do you have any taco-friendly cheese?
Do you want toppings like lime, avocado, or cilantro?
shopping_tips: A lime and cilantro brighten this quickly.
Any taco cheese blend works well.
A jar of salsa is an easy shortcut.
chef_upgrade: Sear the chicken harder for deeper browning.
Warm tortillas in a dry skillet instead of the microwave.
Finish with lime and a pinch of flaky salt.
instructions: Cook the chicken with onion and garlic until fully done.
Season with cumin and paprika if you have them.
Warm tortillas or prepare lettuce wraps.
Fill and top with cheese, avocado, cilantro, or lime if available.

recipes.name: Beef Tacos
questions: Do you have tortillas or lettuce wraps?
Do you want cheese on top?
Do you have lime or hot sauce for finishing?
shopping_tips: Cumin and paprika help a lot.
Bagged shredded lettuce is an easy topping.
chef_upgrade: Brown the beef deeply before stirring.
Let the onion soften well before finishing.
Toast tortillas in a skillet.
instructions: Brown the beef with onion and garlic.
Add cumin and paprika if available and cook for another minute.
Warm tortillas or prepare lettuce wraps.
Assemble and add toppings you have.

recipes.name: Chicken Quesadilla
questions: Do you have a melty cheese on hand?
Would you add onion or peppers?
Do you want sour cream or avocado on the side?
shopping_tips: A melty cheese is the key piece.
Salsa makes it feel more complete.
chef_upgrade: Toast the tortilla in butter for better color.
Cook the filling separately so the tortilla stays crisp.
Rest for a minute before slicing.
instructions: Cook the chicken and any vegetables.
Place a tortilla in a skillet and add cheese plus chicken mixture.
Fold and cook until golden and melty.
Serve with sour cream or avocado if available.

recipes.name: Pasta with Garlic and Olive Oil
questions: Do you have any pasta shape at all?
Do you have parmesan or another cheese?
Would you like some heat from chili flakes?
chef_upgrade: Cook garlic gently so it does not burn.
Use a splash of pasta water to emulsify.
Finish with lemon zest if available.
instructions: Cook pasta until al dente.
Gently sauté garlic in olive oil.
Toss pasta with the garlic oil and a little pasta water if needed.
Finish with chili flakes, cheese, lemon, or parsley if available.

recipes.name: Sausage Tomato Pasta
questions: Do you have fresh tomato or canned tomato?
Do you have an Italian-style cheese?
Would you like basil or chili flakes?
shopping_tips: Crushed or canned tomatoes also work.
A wedge of parmesan lasts a long time.
chef_upgrade: Brown the sausage until you build fond.
Reduce the sauce slightly before adding pasta.
Add basil at the end.
instructions: Cook sausage and break it up in a pan.
Add onion and garlic and cook until softened.
Add tomato and simmer into a quick sauce.
Toss with cooked pasta and finish with cheese or basil if you have them.

recipes.name: Mushroom Pasta
questions: Do you have butter or just oil?
Do you have a cheese to finish it?
Would you like thyme or parsley?
shopping_tips: A little butter helps the sauce.
chef_upgrade: Let mushrooms brown instead of steaming.
Salt mushrooms after they start to color.
Use a little pasta water for silkier texture.
instructions: Cook the pasta.
Sauté mushrooms and garlic in butter or oil until browned.
Toss with pasta.
Finish with cheese and thyme if available.

recipes.name: Greek Salad
questions: Do you have feta?
Do you have olives or red onion?
Would you like a simple oil and vinegar dressing?
chef_upgrade: Salt tomatoes lightly before mixing.
Use good olive oil if you have one.
Add oregano if available.
instructions: Chop cucumber and tomato.
Add feta and red onion if using.
Dress with olive oil and vinegar.
Top with olives if available.

recipes.name: Chicken Curry
questions: Do you have ginger and warm spices?
Do you want rice with it?
Do you have tomato or tomato paste?
shopping_tips: A small spice blend goes a long way.
chef_upgrade: Cook onions longer for deeper flavor.
Toast spices briefly before adding liquid.
Let the curry rest before serving.
instructions: Cook onion and garlic until soft.
Add chicken and brown lightly.
Add spices and tomato if you have them and simmer until chicken is cooked through.
Serve over rice if available.

recipes.name: Chana Masala Style
questions: Do you have canned chickpeas or cooked ones?
Do you want to serve this with rice?
Do you have garlic and ginger?
shopping_tips: Rice makes it a fuller meal.
chef_upgrade: Cook onion until deeply soft.
Mash a few chickpeas into the sauce.
Finish with cilantro if you have it.
instructions: Cook onion until softened.
Add tomato and spices if available.
Stir in chickpeas and simmer until flavorful.
Serve with rice if you have it.

recipes.name: Chicken Stir-Fry
questions: Do you have rice to serve with it?
Do you have garlic or ginger?
Would you like broccoli or another quick vegetable?
shopping_tips: Frozen broccoli is a good backup.
Sesame oil adds a restaurant-style finish.
chef_upgrade: Cook chicken in batches so it browns.
Add sesame oil at the end.
Use very high heat for vegetables.
instructions: Cook the chicken in a hot pan.
Add onion and bell pepper and stir-fry until crisp tender.
Add soy sauce plus garlic or ginger if available.
Serve with rice or finish with sesame oil if you have it.

recipes.name: Vegetable Fried Rice
questions: Do you have leftover rice?
Do you have peas, carrots, or green onion?
Do you want to add another protein?
shopping_tips: Frozen peas and carrots are perfect here.
Green onion adds a nice finish.
chef_upgrade: Use cold rice so it stays separate.
Cook egg first, then add it back later.
Let the rice sit briefly in the pan for better texture.
instructions: Scramble the egg in a hot pan and remove.
Cook vegetables in a little oil if using.
Add rice and soy sauce and stir-fry.
Return the egg to the pan and finish with green onion if available.

recipes.name: Thai Basil Chicken Style
questions: Do you have basil or another fresh herb?
Do you want to serve it with rice?
Do you want it spicy?
chef_upgrade: Cook chicken hard for crispy edges.
Add basil at the very end.
Balance with a touch of sugar if needed.
instructions: Cook chicken with garlic in a hot pan.
Add soy sauce and chili flakes if you like heat.
Stir in basil and bell pepper if available.
Serve over rice.

recipes.name: Teriyaki Salmon Bowl Style
questions: Do you have rice ready or do you need to cook it?
Would you like toppings like cucumber or avocado?
Do you want this sweeter or more savory?
shopping_tips: Sesame seeds are optional but nice.
chef_upgrade: Cook salmon just to medium for better texture.
Reduce the soy glaze slightly.
Top with green onion right before serving.
instructions: Cook the salmon until just done.
Season or glaze lightly with soy sauce.
Serve over rice.
Top with cucumber, avocado, green onion, or sesame seeds if available.
